description: Print the string 'hello world' to stdout.
Print the numbers 1 through 5, each on a separate line.
Print the length of the string 'hello world' (number of characters including the space).
Print the numbers 10, 20, 30 separated by commas (no spaces).
Print 'even' if 42 is even, or 'odd' if 42 is odd.
Print the reverse of the string 'abcdef'.
Print 'yes' if the file /etc/hosts exists, 'no' otherwise.
Print the word 'banana' repeated 3 times separated by spaces.

Print only the third line of the following text: 'alpha
beta
gamma
delta'. Use echo -e to interpret the escape sequences.
Count how many vowels (a, e, i, o, u, case-insensitive) are in the string 'Encyclopedia' and print only the number.
Print the string 'hello' with each character on a separate line.
Print the ASCII value (decimal) of the character 'A'.
Given the string 'foo:bar:baz', replace all colons with newlines and print the result.
